auth_help.relogin_hint: If a bearer token is missing, invalid, or expired, re-authenticate and retry the MCP call.
auth_help.invalid_or_expired_token_message: If credentials look stale, sign in again and resend the bearer token.

concept_notes.scope_note: No-shop style concepts are usually approached through clause-family taxonomy and section text rather than a dedicated normalized agreement field.
This concept is represented primarily through taxonomy-assigned sections, then inspected in section text.
MAE carveouts are often proxied by Material Adverse Effect taxonomy nodes plus section text, especially disproportionate-effects language.
This concept is commonly represented by a specific descendant taxonomy node inside MAE definitions.
Specific performance is typically represented through clause-family assignments and supporting section text.
Antitrust-efforts concepts may span several nearby clause families and should be validated in the returned section text.

tool_limitations.use_when: Use for exploratory lookup when you may combine free-text discovery with filters and only need shallow pagination.
tool_limitations.do_not_use_for: Do not use this tool when you already know the exact structured filters and expect deep pagination; prefer list_agreements.

tool_limitations.use_when: Use for exploratory lookup when you may combine free-text discovery with filters and only need shallow pagination.
tool_limitations.do_not_use_for: Do not treat free-text query hits as normalized extracted facts beyond the documented agreement fields.

tool_limitations.use_when: Use for clause-language retrieval, taxonomy searches, and agreement-section sampling.
tool_limitations.do_not_use_for: Do not use this tool as a source of normalized document-level facts; it returns clause text and metadata attached to matching sections.

tool_limitations.use_when: Use for clause-language retrieval, taxonomy searches, and agreement-section sampling.
tool_limitations.do_not_use_for: Do not assume taxonomy hits are always canonical for the user concept; inspect interpretation notes and concept guidance first.

tool_limitations.use_when: Use when filters are already known and you expect to paginate deeply or export exact result sets.
tool_limitations.do_not_use_for: Do not use this tool for free-text exploration; prefer search_agreements when you are still discovering names or years.

tool_limitations.use_when: Use when you already know the exact agreement UUID and need the agreement payload or XML.
tool_limitations.do_not_use_for: Do not use this tool for bulk discovery or corpus filtering.

tool_limitations.use_when: Use first when you need canonical filter values or need to translate plural catalog groups into retrieval parameter names.
tool_limitations.do_not_use_for: Do not send the pluralized catalog keys directly to retrieval tools; use retrieval_parameter_map to translate them.

tool_limitations.use_when: Use when you know the legal concept but not the right taxonomy id.
tool_limitations.do_not_use_for: Do not treat the top suggestion as automatically canonical; check fit, confidence, and scope_note before relying on it.

tool_limitations.use_when: Use after search_sections when you need a quick excerpt for comparison or quoting.
tool_limitations.do_not_use_for: Do not treat the snippet as the complete section; fetch get_section if exact full context matters.

tool_limitations.use_when: Use when you need a machine-readable guide to tool choice, filters, scopes, and supported workflows.
tool_limitations.do_not_use_for: Do not guess whether a concept is canonical or proxy-based when this tool can tell you directly.

tools.name: search_agreements
tools.selection_hint: Use for exploratory lookup when you may combine free-text discovery with filters and only need shallow pagination.
negative_guidance: Do not use this tool when you already know the exact structured filters and expect deep pagination; prefer list_agreements.
Do not treat free-text query hits as normalized extracted facts beyond the documented agreement fields.

page_size.description: Maximum number of results to return.

query.description: Optional prefix search over target and acquirer names, or a 4-digit year string.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor from a previous agreements listing response.

include_xml.description: When true, include full agreement XML for each result.

target_pe.description: Boolean-like PE filter values encoded as strings.

acquirer_pe.description: Boolean-like PE filter values encoded as strings.

tools.name: search_sections
tools.selection_hint: Use for clause-language retrieval, taxonomy searches, and agreement-section sampling.
negative_guidance: Do not use this tool as a source of normalized document-level facts; it returns clause text and metadata attached to matching sections.
Do not assume taxonomy hits are always canonical for the user concept; inspect interpretation notes and concept guidance first.

target.description: Exact target company names to include.

acquirer.description: Exact acquirer company names to include.

standard_id.description: Clause type taxonomy standard IDs (Clause Type in the Search UI). Parent IDs expand to include descendant taxonomy nodes.

target_pe.description: Boolean-like PE filter values encoded as strings.

acquirer_pe.description: Boolean-like PE filter values encoded as strings.

metadata.description: Agreement metadata fields to include under results[].metadata.

count_mode.description: Count strategy for pagination planning. Use `exact` when planning depends on a guaranteed total count.

page_size.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: list_agreements
tools.selection_hint: Use when filters are already known and you expect to paginate deeply or export exact result sets.
negative_guidance: Do not use this tool for free-text exploration; prefer search_agreements when you are still discovering names or years.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor from a previous list_agreements call.

page_size.description: Maximum number of results to return.

include_xml.description: When true, include full agreement XML. Requires agreements:read_fulltext.

target_pe.description: Boolean-like PE filter values encoded as strings.

acquirer_pe.description: Boolean-like PE filter values encoded as strings.

tools.name: list_agreement_sections
tools.selection_hint: Use after identifying an agreement and before calling get_section on one section UUID.

tools.name: get_agreement
tools.selection_hint: Use when you already know the exact agreement UUID and need the agreement payload or XML.
negative_guidance: Do not use this tool for bulk discovery or corpus filtering.

response_examples.description: Redacted agreement response without fulltext scope.
Full agreement response with fulltext scope.

focus_section_uuid.description: Optional section UUID used when redacting anonymous responses to keep a focused neighborhood visible.

neighbor_sections.description: Number of neighboring sections to include around `focus_section_uuid` when response XML is redacted.

tools.name: get_section
tools.selection_hint: Use when you already have a section UUID and want the exact section payload.

tools.name: get_agreement_tax_clauses
tools.selection_hint: Use for agreement-level tax clause extraction once you know the agreement UUID.

tools.name: get_section_tax_clauses
tools.selection_hint: Use for section-level tax clause extraction when you already have a section UUID.

tools.name: list_filter_options
tools.selection_hint: Use first when you need canonical filter values or need to translate plural catalog groups into retrieval parameter names.
negative_guidance: Do not send the pluralized catalog keys directly to retrieval tools; use retrieval_parameter_map to translate them.

response_examples.description: Filter catalog response with mapping metadata.

tools.name: suggest_clause_families
tools.selection_hint: Use when you know the legal concept but not the right taxonomy id.
negative_guidance: Do not treat the top suggestion as automatically canonical; check fit, confidence, and scope_note before relying on it.

response_examples.description: Ranked taxonomy suggestions for a concept.

matches.label: Material Adverse Effect
matches.scope_note: This taxonomy node is a reasonable proxy and may be broader or narrower than the requested concept.
matches.reason: Matched concept tokens and clause-family synonyms.

tools.name: get_section_snippet
tools.selection_hint: Use after search_sections when you need a quick excerpt for comparison or quoting.
negative_guidance: Do not treat the snippet as the complete section; fetch get_section if exact full context matters.

content.snippet: ...disproportionate effects on the Company relative to others in the industry...

tools.name: get_server_metrics
tools.selection_hint: Use for operational monitoring and to see which MCP tools are slow or error-prone.

response_examples.description: Metrics snapshot with one recorded tool.

tools.name: get_server_capabilities
tools.selection_hint: Use when you need a machine-readable guide to tool choice, filters, scopes, and supported workflows.
negative_guidance: Do not guess whether a concept is canonical or proxy-based when this tool can tell you directly.

tools.name: get_clause_taxonomy
tools.selection_hint: Use when you need valid standard_id values for section taxonomy filtering.

tools.name: get_tax_clause_taxonomy
tools.selection_hint: Use when you need tax-clause taxonomy ids before calling a tax-clause retrieval tool.

tools.name: get_counsel_catalog
tools.selection_hint: Use when you need canonical firm names before counsel-filtered agreement or section retrieval.

tools.name: get_naics_catalog
tools.selection_hint: Use when you need canonical industry labels before industry-filtered retrieval.

tools.name: get_agreements_summary
tools.selection_hint: Use for top-level corpus sizing before deeper analysis.

tools.name: get_agreement_trends
tools.selection_hint: Use for aggregated corpus analytics rather than document retrieval.
